explanation:
**Official procedures** for vehicle transfers in Argentina have costs, timelines, and requirements defined by each agency (RENAPER, DNRPA, AFIP, Immigration, Civil Registry, etc.). This calculator estimates your total cost and required documents.

## How It's Calculated

Used car transfer cost breaks down into registration fees + provincial taxes + state stamps · free and fast.

This calculator applies the formula with your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an estimate for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a specialized professional. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much does it cost to transfer a used car in Argentina?
faq.a: Total cost varies by province but typically includes DNRPA registration fees + provincial stamp tax (1-2%) + gestor fees if needed. Use this calculator with your car value and state to get an exact estimate.

faq.q: What is included in the transfer cost?
faq.a: DNRPA (National Vehicle Registry) fees + provincial stamp tax + documentation processing. Gestor fees ($50k-150k ARS) are optional but speed up the process.

faq.q: Do I need to hire a gestor (agent) to transfer a car?
faq.a: No, it's optional. You can do it yourself through DNRPA, but a gestor can accelerate the process and handle documentation.

faq.q: How long does a used car transfer take?
faq.a: Typically 2-7 business days if all documentation is in order. May be longer depending on your province and DNRPA workload.

faq.q: What documents do I need for a used car transfer?
faq.a: Original title (escritura), green or blue ID card (cédula), debt verification, clean title check, and valid ID. Requirements may vary by province.

faq.q: Are there any exemptions from transfer fees?
faq.a: Yes. Direct family inheritance and vehicles for people with disabilities may qualify for exemptions or reduced fees. Check your provincial requirements.

faq.q: Is there a cost difference between CABA and Buenos Aires Province?
faq.a: Yes. Stamp tax rates and DNRPA fees vary by jurisdiction. CABA and PBA have different fee structures—use your specific province for accurate pricing.

faq.q: Can I transfer a car with an outstanding loan?
faq.a: Typically no. The vehicle must be free of debt or liens. Your bank will hold the title until the loan is fully paid off.

faq.q: Do I need an appointment to transfer a car at DNRPA?
faq.a: Yes, most DNRPA offices require an appointment. You can book online at argentina.gob.ar or your provincial registry website.
